Convert this natural language question to a SQL query for the 'pets_1' database.
Database schema: Table Student: [StuID (number), LName (text), Fname (text), Age (number), Sex (text), Major (number), Advisor (number), city_code (text)] | Table Has_Pet: [StuID (number), PetID (number)] | Table Pets: [PetID (number), PetType (text), pet_age (number), weight (number)]

Question: Find the last name of the student who has a cat that is age 3.

When ready, output the final SQL enclosed in <answer> and </answer> tags. Only output SELECT statements.
gold_sql: SELECT T1.lname FROM student AS T1 JOIN has_pet AS T2 ON T1.stuid  =  T2.stuid JOIN pets AS T3 ON T3.petid  =  T2.petid WHERE T3.pet_age  =  3 AND T3.pettype  =  'cat'
answer: SELECT T1.lname FROM student AS T1 JOIN has_pet AS T2 ON T1.stuid  =  T2.stuid JOIN pets AS T3 ON T3.petid  =  T2.petid WHERE T3.pet_age  =  3 AND T3.pettype  =  'cat'

question: Find the name of the makers that produced some cars in the year of 1970?
query:
Convert this natural language question to a SQL query for the 'car_1' database.

gold_sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Maker FROM CAR_MAKERS AS T1 JOIN MODEL_LIST AS T2 ON T1.Id  =  T2.Maker JOIN CAR_NAMES AS T3 ON T2.model  =  T3.model JOIN CARS_DATA AS T4 ON T3.MakeId  =  T4.id WHERE T4.year  =  '1970';

question: Which are the car makers which produce at least 2 models and more than 3 car makers ? List the id and the maker .
query:
Convert this natural language question to a SQL query for the 'car_1' database.
Database schema: Table continents: [ContId (number), Continent (text)] | Table countries: [CountryId (number), CountryName (text), Continent (number)] | Table car_makers: [Id (number), Maker (text), FullName (text), Country (text)] | Table model_list: [ModelId (number), Maker (number), Model (text)] | Table car_names: [MakeId (number), Model (text), Make (text)] | Table cars_data: [Id (number), MPG (text), Cylinders (number), Edispl (number), Horsepower (text), Weight (number), Accelerate (number), Year (number)]

Question: Which are the car makers which produce at least 2 models and more than 3 car makers ? List the id and the maker .

gold_sql: select t1.id ,  t1.maker from car_makers as t1 join model_list as t2 on t1.id  =  t2.maker group by t1.id having count(*)  >=  2 intersect select t1.id ,  t1.maker from car_makers as t1 join model_list as t2 on t1.id  =  t2.maker join car_names as t3 on t2.model  =  t3.model group by t1.id having count(*)  >  3;

question: What are the ids and makers of all car makers that produce at least 2 models and make more than 3 cars?
query:
Convert this natural language question to a SQL query for the 'car_1' database.

question: What are the id and names of the countries which have more than 3 car makers or produce the 'fiat' model?
query:
Convert this natural language question to a SQL query for the 'car_1' database.
Database schema: Table continents: [ContId (number), Continent (text)] | Table countries: [CountryId (number), CountryName (text), Continent (number)] | Table car_makers: [Id (number), Maker (text), FullName (text), Country (text)] | Table model_list: [ModelId (number), Maker (number), Model (text)] | Table car_names: [MakeId (number), Model (text), Make (text)] | Table cars_data: [Id (number), MPG (text), Cylinders (number), Edispl (number), Horsepower (text), Weight (number), Accelerate (number), Year (number)]

Question: What are the id and names of the countries which have more than 3 car makers or produce the 'fiat' model?

Question: What is the semester which most student registered in? Show both the name and the id.

When ready, output the final SQL enclosed in <answer> and </answer> tags. Only output SELECT statements.
gold_sql: SELECT T1.semester_name ,  T1.semester_id FROM Semesters AS T1 JOIN Student_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.semester_id  =  T2.semester_id GROUP BY T1.semester_id ORDER BY count(*) DESC LIMIT 1
answer: SELECT T1.semester_name ,  T1.semester_id FROM Semesters AS T1 JOIN Student_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.semester_id  =  T2.semester_id GROUP BY T1.semester_id ORDER BY count(*) DESC LIMIT 1

Convert this natural language question to a SQL query for the 'network_1' database.
Database schema: Table Highschooler: [ID (number), name (text), grade (number)] | Table Friend: [student_id (number), friend_id (number)] | Table Likes: [student_id (number), liked_id (number)]

Question: What is the name of the high schooler who has the greatest number of likes?

When ready, output the final SQL enclosed in <answer> and </answer> tags. Only output SELECT statements.
gold_sql: SELECT T2.name FROM Likes AS T1 JOIN Highschooler AS T2 ON T1.student_id  =  T2.id GROUP BY T1.student_id ORDER BY count(*) DESC LIMIT 1
answer: SELECT T2.name FROM Likes AS T1 JOIN Highschooler AS T2 ON T1.student_id  =  T2.id GROUP BY T1.student_id ORDER BY count(*) DESC LIMIT 1
